description: PUH-BrianMartell 20260413_PUH_Theorem191_Formal_Abstract.tex-Updated Abstract

PUH-BrianMartell-PUH Theorem 191 (The Schrödinger Equation from E8 Bow Wave Propagation) onto Zenodo with DOI 10.5281/zenodo.19560904 and also onto Academia. In plain English: the Schrödinger equation iħ dψ/dt = Hψ is not a postulate or an analogy. It is the exact wave equation for an E8 bow wave propagating through the Phase I condensate. The wave function ψ(x,t) is the complex amplitude of that real physical bow wave (pressure oscillation in the E8 lattice). The free equation comes directly from the non-relativistic bow-wave dispersion relation ω = ħk²/(2m). The potential V(x) is a local variation in E8 lattice tension λ(x) that shifts the bow-wave phase. The imaginary unit i is the U(1) phase rotation generator — quantum mechanics is complex because the bow wave is a U(1) Cartan phase-mode oscillation.
